MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Mixing and Effects

Source: MOTU product page

On-Board DSP Mixer

The UltraLite-mk3's built-in DSP mixer operates at 32-bit floating-point precision entirely in hardware. Any input can be routed to any output at zero latency — the computer is completely bypassed in the signal path. This is the foundation of latency-free monitoring.

Zero-Latency Monitor Mix

When a vocalist is recording through the SM58, the standard DAW monitoring path (mic → MOTU → computer → DAW → MOTU → headphones) adds audible delay. The CueMix FX solution:

  1. In CueMix FX, create a direct send from Input 1 (SM58) to the Phones output bus — zero computer involvement.
  2. In Ableton, set the vocal Audio Track's Monitor to Off (or Auto with recording enabled). This prevents the computer-processed signal from doubling with the hardware monitor signal.
  3. The vocalist hears themselves with zero perceptible delay.

Separate Parallel Mixes

  • Mix 1 (Main): flat, accurate monitoring signal to the HS7 studio monitors. Represents what's actually in the session without enhancement.
  • Mix 2 (Phones/Cue): customized headphone mix for the vocalist — their own voice louder (+6 dB relative to backing track), with a touch of CueMix Classic Reverb for comfort and feel during recording.

Standalone Mixer Mode

All CueMix FX mix settings are saved to the MOTU's internal flash memory. Disconnect the computer entirely — the UltraLite-mk3 operates as a standalone hardware mixer, retaining all your routing, levels, and effects settings. This is useful for live performance where a laptop isn't required.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
